results.rendered_html:
cūṣ cl. 1. ˚ṣati, to suck, suck out,  Dhātup. xvii, 22  :
Pass. ˚ṣyate, to be sucked up or dried up (by internal inflammation),  Suśr. i f.  :
Caus. ˚ṣayati, to suck up, iv ;
cf. saṃ-.

results.rendered_html:
cūṣ , cūṣati saugen, aussaugen (pāne)  DHĀTUP. 17,22 . pass. sieden, wallen (in einer Wunde, Geschwulst u. s. w.): na dahyante na cūṣyante (vraṇāḥ)  SUŚR. 1,103,17 . uṣyate cūṣyate dahyate pacyata iva vastiḥ 262,13 . — caus. aufsaugen  SUŚR. 2,33,16 .
— Vgl. cūṣya, coṣa, coṣya, cuścyūṣā.
— ā s. ācūṣaṇa.
— sam pass. sieden, wallen: saṃcūṣyate dahyate ca  SUŚR. 2,486,10 .
